prologue.title: How to Use This Dictionary
prologue.intro: Welcome to the Read Fluently Dictionary. This dictionary is designed to help you understand words as you read.

parts.name: Word
parts.explanation: This is the main word you are learning.

parts.name: Part of Speech
parts.explanation: This tells you how the word works in a sentence.

parts.name: Syllables
parts.explanation: This shows how the word can be broken into smaller sound parts.

parts.name: Definition
parts.explanation: This explains what the word means in simple language.

parts.name: Example Sentence
parts.explanation: This shows how the word is used in a sentence.

prologue.searchHelp: Use the search button to look for a word quickly. If the word is not here yet, you may check another trusted dictionary while this one continues to grow.

words.definition: The power or skill to do something.
words.example: Her reading ability improved with practice.

words.definition: Having the power or skill to do something.
words.example: She is able to read the whole story.

words.definition: Connected with or relating to something.
words.example: We read a story about animals.

words.definition: In a higher place than something else.
words.example: The picture hung above the desk.

words.definition: To agree to take something that is offered.
words.example: She accepted the teacher's help.

words.definition: A set of steps or rules used to solve a problem or make a decision.
words.example: A video app uses an algorithm to suggest what you may want to watch next.

words.definition: Technology that helps computers perform tasks such as analysing information, finding patterns, making predictions, or generating responses.
words.example: Artificial intelligence can help organise large amounts of information.

words.definition: The people who watch, read, or listen to something.
words.example: The teacher created videos for a large audience of learners.

words.definition: What you already know about a topic before you read or learn more about it.
words.example: Her background knowledge helped her understand the story.

words.definition: Being focused and involved while learning or reading.
words.example: Being engaged helps learners understand more.

words.definition: The way a company earns money and keeps operating.
words.example: Subscriptions are one kind of business model.

words.definition: Stopping to fix confusion when something does not make sense.
words.example: Clarifying helped her understand the difficult sentence.

words.definition: A set of instructions written for a computer to follow.
words.example: The program works because the code tells it what to do.

words.definition: A percentage of money taken from a sale or payment.
words.example: The platform earns a commission from every product sold.

words.definition: The ability to understand what you read or hear.
words.example: Good readers focus on comprehension, not just saying the words.

words.definition: A person who uses, buys, watches, reads, or listens to what others provide.
words.example: A viewer on a video platform is a consumer.

words.definition: The legal protection given to creators over their original work.
words.example: Songs and books are protected by copyright.

words.definition: A person who makes something valuable such as a video, lesson, book, song, or product.
words.example: A teacher who makes online lessons is a creator.

words.definition: Carefully analysing and evaluating information before accepting it as true.
words.example: Critical thinking helps readers evaluate ideas in a text.

words.definition: Facts or information collected for use, study, or decision-making.
words.example: A system may use data to find patterns.

words.definition: Parts of a system that rely on other parts in order to work properly.
words.example: If one part changes, broken dependencies can cause problems.

words.definition: A reference book or tool that explains the meanings of words.
words.example: She checked the dictionary to understand the new word.

words.definition: Very ready and excited to do something.
words.example: The learners were eager to start the science experiment.

words.definition: To get something because you worked for it.
words.example: She worked hard to earn a good mark.

words.definition: The outside border of something.
words.example: The cup was close to the edge of the table.

words.definition: To change or improve writing or work by correcting it.
words.example: He had to edit his paragraph before handing it in.

words.definition: The process of learning at school or from study.
words.example: Education helps people build knowledge and skills.

words.definition: A result or change caused by something else.
words.example: Too little sleep can have a bad effect on learning.

words.definition: Energy and determination used when trying to achieve something.
words.example: Effort helps learners improve their skills.

words.definition: Old or fairly old.
words.example: The boy helped the elderly woman carry her bag.

words.definition: To choose someone by voting.
words.example: The class will elect a new monitor.

words.definition: A form of energy that gives power to lights and machines.
words.example: The storm caused the electricity to go off.

words.definition: A basic part of something.
words.example: Water is an important element of life.

words.definition: To give someone support or confidence.
words.example: The teacher used kind words to encourage the class.

words.definition: A person or group that is against another.
words.example: In the story, the hero faced a powerful enemy.

words.definition: Strength and power to do work or move.
words.example: Healthy food gives your body energy.

words.definition: Being focused and mentally involved while reading or learning.
words.example: She stayed engaged while reading the chapter.

words.definition: Being fully focused, involved, and mentally active while reading or learning.
words.example: Engagement helps readers understand more deeply.

words.definition: Very large.
words.example: They saw an enormous elephant at the park.

words.definition: To go into a place.
words.example: The learners lined up before they could enter the hall.

words.definition: The same in number, amount, or value.
words.example: Two halves make equal parts.

words.definition: To get away from a place or danger.
words.example: The bird tried to escape from the cage.

words.definition: Very important and necessary.
words.example: Water is essential for all living things.

words.definition: To make a careful guess.
words.example: Please estimate how many books are on the shelf.

words.definition: Something that happens, especially something important.
words.example: Sports day is a big school event.

words.definition: Facts, examples, or information that support an idea.
words.example: The writer used evidence to support the main point.

words.definition: To look at something carefully.
words.example: The learners examined the insect with a magnifying glass.

words.definition: Something that shows what another thing is like.
words.example: The teacher gave an example of a proper sentence.

words.definition: Very good.
words.example: She did an excellent job on her project.

words.definition: Not including.
words.example: Everyone came to school except Musa.

words.definition: To give one thing and receive another.
words.example: The learners exchanged books after reading.

words.definition: Very happy and full of interest about something.
words.example: The class was excited about the trip.

words.definition: Activity that helps keep the body strong and healthy.
words.example: Running is a good form of exercise.

words.definition: Very tired.
words.example: After the race, the runners felt exhausted.

words.definition: To be real or to be present.
words.example: Many kinds of plants exist in the forest.

words.definition: To grow bigger or make something bigger.
words.example: Heat can expand some materials.

words.definition: Something that happens to you and teaches you something.
words.example: The camp was a fun learning experience.

words.definition: To make something clear by telling more about it.
words.example: Please explain how you got your answer.

words.definition: To look around and learn more about something.
words.example: The class went outside to explore the school garden.

words.definition: Reading or speaking with the correct tone, pauses, and feeling.
words.example: Good readers use expression when reading aloud.

words.definition: More than what is usual or needed.
words.example: She brought an extra pencil to class.

words.definition: A statement that can be proven true with evidence.
words.example: Water freezes at zero degrees Celsius is a fact.

words.definition: Not strong, clear, or easy to notice.
words.example: We heard a faint sound from outside.

words.definition: Loyal and trustworthy.
words.example: The dog was a faithful friend.

words.definition: Well known to you.
words.example: The song sounded familiar to the learners.

words.definition: Known by many people.
words.example: Table Mountain is a famous place in South Africa.

words.definition: An important part of something.
words.example: A beak is a feature of a bird.

words.definition: Strong, wild, or dangerous.
words.example: The lion gave a fierce roar.

words.definition: Last.
words.example: The final question was the hardest.

words.definition: A small storage device used to save and move digital files.
words.example: He saved the work on a flash drive.

words.definition: Reading smoothly, clearly, and with natural expression.
words.example: Fluent reading makes the story easier to understand.

words.definition: The ability to read smoothly, accurately, and with expression.
words.example: Practising reading helps improve fluency.

words.definition: To pay close attention to something.
words.example: Try to focus on the main idea.

words.definition: Lucky.
words.example: We were fortunate that the rain stopped.

words.definition: The basic skills or base that support further learning.
words.example: Listening skills form the foundation of reading.

words.definition: Easy to break or damage.
words.example: Glass is fragile, so handle it carefully.

words.definition: Happening often.
words.example: Frequent practice helps learners improve.

words.definition: Upset because something is difficult or not working.
words.example: He felt frustrated when he could not solve the problem.

words.definition: The job or purpose of something.
words.example: The function of roots is to hold the plant and take in water.

words.definition: The time that is still to come.
words.example: Learners often wonder what the future will look like.

words.definition: To bring things or people together.
words.example: The children gathered around the teacher.

words.definition: Willing to share and give kindly.
words.example: It was generous of her to share her lunch.

words.definition: Kind, calm, and not rough.
words.example: Use a gentle voice when speaking to younger children.

words.definition: A list of important words with their meanings.
words.example: The glossary explains difficult words in the chapter.

words.definition: Something you want to achieve.
words.example: Her goal was to finish the book by Friday.

words.definition: Feeling thankful.
words.example: I am grateful for your help.

words.definition: The belief that abilities can improve through effort and practice.
words.example: A growth mindset helps learners stay motivated.

words.definition: To show or lead someone in the right direction.
words.example: The map will guide us to the museum.

words.definition: Something you do regularly.
words.example: Reading every day is a good habit.

words.definition: Causing damage or hurt.
words.example: Too much smoke can be harmful to health.

words.definition: The time when crops are picked and collected.
words.example: Farmers work hard during the harvest.

words.definition: To pause because you are unsure.
words.example: Do not hesitate to ask for help.

words.definition: Truthful and not trying to cheat.
words.example: It is important to be honest in your work.

words.definition: Going from side to side, not up and down.
words.example: Draw a horizontal line across the page.

words.definition: A thought, plan, or suggestion in your mind.
words.example: She had a good idea for the class project.

words.definition: To recognise or name something correctly.
words.example: The learners had to identify the main idea of the passage.

words.definition: To pay no attention to something or someone.
words.example: Do not ignore the instructions on the worksheet.

words.definition: A picture or visual representation of something.
words.example: The image in the book helped the learners understand the topic.

words.definition: To form a picture or idea in your mind.
words.example: Try to imagine the scene while you read the story.

words.definition: Happening at once or without delay.
words.example: The teacher needed an immediate answer.

words.definition: Having great value or meaning.
words.example: It is important to listen carefully in class.

words.definition: To make something better or become better.
words.example: Daily practice will help improve your reading.

words.definition: To have something as part of a whole.
words.example: The project must include a title and pictures.

words.definition: To become more or make something more.
words.example: Exercise can increase your energy.

words.definition: Able to do things by yourself.
words.example: She became a more independent reader.

words.definition: To show or point out something.
words.example: The graph can indicate a change in temperature.

words.definition: One single person.
words.example: Each individual in the group had a task.

words.definition: A type of work or business activity.
words.example: Technology is an important industry.

words.definition: Facts or details about something.
words.example: The text gave useful information about animals.

words.definition: One of the things used to make food or another mixture.
words.example: Flour is one ingredient in bread.

words.definition: To live in a place.
words.example: Many animals inhabit the forest.

words.definition: Happening at the beginning.
words.example: Her initial answer was not correct.

words.definition: A new idea, method, or invention that improves something.
words.example: Innovation can make learning tools more useful.

words.definition: To hurt someone physically.
words.example: Be careful not to injure yourself during the game.

words.definition: To look at something carefully.
words.example: The teacher asked the class to inspect the leaf closely.

words.definition: To fill someone with the wish to do something well.
words.example: The story can inspire learners to keep trying.

words.definition: To put something in place so it can be used.
words.example: They had to install the new software on the computer.

words.definition: Happening right away.
words.example: She wanted an instant answer.

words.definition: In place of something else.
words.example: He read the book instead of watching television.

words.definition: A direction or order telling you what to do.
words.example: Read the instruction before starting the activity.

words.definition: A tool or device used for a special purpose, especially in music or science.
words.example: A thermometer is an instrument used to measure temperature.

words.definition: Good at learning, understanding, and solving problems.
words.example: The learner gave an intelligent answer.

words.definition: A feeling of wanting to know more about something.
words.example: He showed great interest in science.

words.definition: To stop someone while they are speaking or doing something.
words.example: Please do not interrupt when someone is reading.

words.definition: To present something or someone for the first time.
words.example: The teacher will introduce a new topic today.

words.definition: To look into something carefully to learn more about it.
words.example: The class will investigate how plants grow.

words.definition: A request asking someone to come or take part.
words.example: She received an invitation to the school event.

words.definition: To include or require something as part of an activity.
words.example: The experiment will involve water and sunlight.

words.definition: A problem or topic that needs attention.
words.example: The class discussed the issue of littering.

words.definition: An experience that happens over time as you move forward and learn.
words.example: Learning to read well is a journey.

words.definition: To form an opinion about something or someone.
words.example: Do not judge a book by its cover.

words.definition: Fair treatment according to what is right.
words.example: The story teaches an important lesson about justice.

words.definition: Very interested or eager.
words.example: She is keen to learn more about space.

words.definition: One of the organs in the body that cleans the blood.
words.example: The kidneys help remove waste from the body.

words.definition: What you know because you have learned it.
words.example: Reading builds knowledge about the world.

words.definition: A word, tag, or short description used to identify something.
words.example: Put a label on your folder with your name.

words.definition: Words and sentences used for speaking, reading, and writing.
words.example: English is one language used at school.

words.definition: The common ways words and sentences are used in a language.
words.example: Children learn language patterns by listening to people speak.

words.definition: Bigger in size.
words.example: The blue box is larger than the red one.

words.definition: To start something such as a product, plan, or event.
words.example: The school will launch a new reading programme.

words.definition: One thickness or level placed over or under another.
words.example: The Earth has several layers.

words.definition: A person who guides or directs others.
words.example: The group chose a leader for the project.

words.definition: To gain knowledge or skill.
words.example: We learn new words by reading often.

words.definition: A person who is learning.
words.example: Each learner brought a book to class.

words.definition: A period of learning or something taught.
words.example: Today’s lesson was about fractions.

words.definition: A stage, amount, or height.
words.example: This book is at the right level for Grade 5.

words.definition: A place where books and other reading materials are kept.
words.example: The learners visited the library after break.

words.definition: Official permission to use, distribute, stream, or sell content.
words.example: The company needed a licence to stream the film.

words.definition: A person who continues learning throughout life.
words.example: Reading can help someone become a lifelong learner.

words.definition: To pay attention to sounds or spoken words.
words.example: Please listen carefully to the instructions.

words.definition: A reading strategy where you listen to someone read while following the words in the text.
words.example: The class used listen and follow to practise reading.

words.definition: Paying careful attention to spoken words in order to understand them.
words.example: Listening to stories helps learners understand language.

words.definition: To find the position of something.
words.example: Use the map to locate the river.

words.definition: A clear system of rules and reasoning that helps things make sense.
words.example: Good programming depends on logic.

words.definition: A device that uses power to do work.
words.example: The washing machine cleans clothes.

words.definition: The most important point in a paragraph or text.
words.example: The main idea tells you what the paragraph is mostly about.

words.definition: To keep something in good condition.
words.example: It is important to maintain your school books.

words.definition: Very important or large.
words.example: Reading is a major skill in school.

words.definition: The message or idea that a writer is trying to communicate.
words.example: She stopped reading to think about the meaning.

words.definition: To find the size, amount, or length of something.
words.example: Use a ruler to measure the line.

words.definition: The ability to remember information.
words.example: Practice helps improve memory.

words.definition: Thinking about how you think and learn.
words.example: Metacognition helps readers monitor their understanding.

words.definition: A way of doing something.
words.example: This method makes solving problems easier.

words.definition: The centre part of something.
words.example: The answer is in the middle of the paragraph.

words.definition: The part of a person that thinks and understands.
words.example: Reading helps grow your mind.

words.definition: Something that is wrong or done incorrectly.
words.example: Making a mistake helps you learn.

words.definition: An example or demonstration of how something works.
words.example: The teacher showed a model of good reading.

words.definition: When a teacher or skilled reader demonstrates fluent reading for learners.
words.example: Modelled reading helps learners hear how good reading sounds.

words.definition: To check and keep track of something.
words.example: Good readers monitor their understanding.

words.definition: To encourage someone to take action.
words.example: The teacher tries to motivate learners.

words.definition: The act of changing position.
words.example: The movement of air is called wind.

words.definition: Something that comes from nature, not made by humans.
words.example: Plants are part of the natural world.

words.definition: Something that is needed.
words.example: Water is necessary for life.

words.definition: When a platform becomes more valuable as more people use it.
words.example: Messaging apps often grow through network effects.

words.definition: The brain’s ability to change and grow stronger through practice and learning.
words.example: Neuroplasticity helps readers improve with practice.

words.definition: Special cells in the brain that send and receive information.
words.example: Neurons help the brain learn new skills.

words.definition: To see or become aware of something.
words.example: Did you notice the new word?

words.definition: A thing that can be seen or touched.
words.example: The object on the table is a book.

words.definition: To watch something carefully.
words.example: The learners observe how plants grow.

words.definition: To happen.
words.example: Rain can occur in summer.

words.definition: A belief or feeling that someone has about something.
words.example: Saying one season is the best is an opinion.

words.definition: A chance to do something.
words.example: Reading gives you the opportunity to learn.

words.definition: To arrange things in order.
words.example: Organise your notes before studying.

words.definition: The legal right to control, use, or protect something that belongs to you.
words.example: Ownership matters when creative work is shared online.

words.definition: Explaining an idea in your own words.
words.example: Paraphrasing helps you check if you understood the chapter.

words.definition: Connections between brain cells that allow information to travel.
words.example: Practice strengthens the pathways used for reading.

words.definition: A repeated design or sequence.
words.example: The numbers follow a pattern.

words.definition: To carry out an action or task.
words.example: The class will perform a play.

words.definition: Continuing to try even when something is difficult.
words.example: Perseverance helps learners succeed in hard tasks.

words.definition: Related to the body or real things you can touch.
words.example: Exercise is important for physical health.

words.definition: A way to organise what you will do.
words.example: Make a plan before starting your project.

words.definition: A digital system that connects people or groups and helps them interact.
words.example: A shopping app can be a platform.

words.definition: The place where something is located.
words.example: The position of the sun changes during the day.

words.definition: Doing something again and again to improve.
words.example: Practice helps you read better.

words.definition: Being exact, careful, and accurate.
words.example: Computers depend on precision.

words.definition: Making a thoughtful guess about what might happen next or what a text may say.
words.example: Predicting keeps readers actively thinking.

words.definition: To get ready for something.
words.example: Prepare for the test by studying.

words.definition: Looking at the title, headings, or pictures before reading to prepare your mind.
words.example: Previewing helps readers get ready for the text.

words.definition: A series of steps to achieve something.
words.example: Reading is a process of understanding.

words.definition: To make or create something.
words.example: Plants produce oxygen.

words.definition: The process of writing instructions that tell computers what to do.
words.example: Programming allows people to build apps and tools.

words.definition: Improvement over time.
words.example: She made great progress in reading.

words.definition: To keep something safe.
words.example: Trees help protect the soil.

words.definition: The reason why something is created or written.
words.example: The author’s purpose was to inform the reader.

words.definition: How good or bad something is.
words.example: The quality of her work improved over time.

words.definition: The amount or number of something.
words.example: The recipe tells us the quantity of flour to use.

words.definition: One of four equal parts.
words.example: A quarter of the class was absent.

words.definition: Something you ask in order to get information.
words.example: She asked a good question in class.

words.definition: Asking yourself questions while reading to help you understand.
words.example: Questioning helps readers stay mentally active.

words.definition: A line of people waiting for something.
words.example: The learners stood in a queue outside the hall.

words.definition: Fast.
words.example: She gave a quick answer.

words.definition: Making very little noise.
words.example: The library was quiet during reading time.

words.definition: To a fairly large degree or amount.
words.example: The test was quite difficult.

words.definition: Words taken exactly from what someone said or wrote.
words.example: She copied the quotation carefully.

words.definition: Asking yourself questions while reading to help you understand.
words.example: Questioning helps readers stay mentally active.

words.definition: A system that helps decide what users should see next based on behaviour or interests.
words.example: A recommendation system may suggest videos or songs.

words.definition: Reading the same passage several times to improve fluency.
words.example: Repeated reading helped her become smoother and more confident.

words.definition: Reading a sentence or paragraph again to improve understanding.
words.example: Rereading helped him understand the difficult paragraph.

words.definition: Payments made to creators or rights holders when their work is used.
words.example: Musicians may earn royalties when songs are streamed.

words.definition: Noticing when something does not make sense and taking steps to understand it.
words.example: Self-checking helped him realise he needed to pause and think.

words.definition: Checking your own understanding and fixing confusion when needed.
words.example: Self-monitoring helped me notice that I needed to reread.

words.definition: Reducing your reading speed to understand difficult information better.
words.example: Slowing down helped her understand the hard section.

words.definition: A plan or method used to achieve a goal or solve a problem.
words.example: Readers use strategy to understand difficult text.

words.definition: A regular payment for continued access to a service or platform.
words.example: Many streaming platforms use a subscription model.

words.definition: Explaining the most important idea in your own words.
words.example: Summarising helps readers remember what they read.

words.definition: Extra information that explains or proves the main idea.
words.example: Supporting details help the reader understand the main idea.

words.definition: Tools, systems, and machines created to solve problems or make life easier.
words.example: Phones and computers are examples of technology.

words.definition: Creating pictures in your mind while reading or listening.
words.example: Visualising helps readers understand scenes better.

words.definition: The collection of words a person knows and understands.
words.example: Reading helps grow vocabulary.
